questions.question:
Consider two well-formed formulas in prepositional logic.
\(F1: \; P\Rightarrow eg P\)
\(F2: \;( P\Rightarrow eg P)\vee ( eg P\Rightarrow P)\)
Which of the following statements is correct?

(GATE CSE 2001)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following relations:

  
R1(a,b) iff (a+b) is even over the set of integers
R2(a,b) iff (a+b) is odd over the set of integers
R3(a,b) iff a.b > 0 over the set of non-zero rational numbers
R4(a,b) iff |a - b| <= 2 over the set of natural numbers
Which of the following statements is correct?

(GATE CSE 2001)
options:
A.

R1 and R2 are equivalence relations, R3 and R4 are not

B.

R1 and R3 are equivalence relations, R2 and R4 are not

C.

R1 and R4 are equivalence relations, R2 and R3 are not

D.

R1, R2, R3 and R4 are all equivalence relations
questions.correct_answer:
B.

R1 and R3 are equivalence relations, R2 and R4 are not
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Choose the correct alternatives (More than one may be correct).
A graph is planar if and only if,

(GATE CSE 1990)
options:
A.

It does not contain subgraphs homeomorphic to \(k_{5}\) and \(k_{3, 3}\).

B.

It does not contain subgraphs isomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\).

C.

It does not contain a subgraph isomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\)

D.

It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\).
questions.correct_answer:
D.

It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\).
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Answer the following:
Which of the following statements are FALSE?

(GATE CSE 1989)
options:
A.

For poisson distribution, the mean is twice the variance.

B.

In queuing theory, if arrivals occur according to poisson distribution, then the inter-arrival time is exponentially distributed.

C.

The distribution of waiting time is independent of the service discipline used in selecting the waiting customers for service.

D.

If the time between successive arrivals is exponential, then the time between the occurences of every third arrival is also exponential.
questions.correct_answer:
A.

For poisson distribution, the mean is twice the variance.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
If \(f(x_{i}).f(x_{i+1}) \lt 0\) then

(GATE CSE 1987)
options:
A.

There must be a root of f(x) between \(x_i\) and \(x_{i+1}\)

B.

There need not be a root of f(x) between \(x_{i}\) and \(x_{i+1}\).

C.

There fourth derivative of f(x) with respect to x vanishes at \(x_{i}\).

D.

The fourth derivative of f(x) with respect to x vanishes at \(x_{i+1}\).
questions.correct_answer:
A.

There must be a root of f(x) between \(x_i\) and \(x_{i+1}\)
